name: Ashwagandha
description: Ashwagandha is one of Ayurveda's most valued adaptogenic herbs, traditionally used to improve vitality, reduce stress, and support overall wellness.
habitat: Dry tropical and subtropical climates with sandy well-drained soil.
interestingFact: Its name means 'Smell of the Horse', symbolizing strength and vitality.
research: Modern research focuses on its stress-reducing and cognitive-support properties.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional during pregnancy or if taking thyroid medication.

name: Neem
description: Neem is known as the 'Village Pharmacy' because almost every part of the tree has traditional medicinal value.
habitat: Warm tropical climates with full sunlight.
interestingFact: Neem trees can live for more than 200 years.
research: Widely researched for antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
precautions: Neem oil should not be consumed unless advised by a healthcare professional.

name: Tulsi
description: Tulsi, or Holy Basil, is regarded as the Queen of Herbs and holds both medicinal and spiritual significance.
habitat: Warm tropical environments with fertile soil.
interestingFact: Tulsi is traditionally grown in courtyards across India.
research: Modern studies investigate its antioxidant and immune-supporting effects.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional if taking blood-thinning medication.

name: Aloe Vera
description: Aloe Vera is valued for its soothing gel, traditionally used to support skin health and digestive wellness.
habitat: Dry climates with sandy, well-drained soil.
interestingFact: Ancient Egyptians called it the 'Plant of Immortality'.
research: Widely studied for skincare and digestive applications.
precautions: The latex portion should not be consumed in excess.

name: Turmeric
description: Turmeric is one of Ayurveda's most celebrated medicinal herbs, recognized for its vibrant rhizome and traditional wellness applications.
habitat: Warm, humid tropical climates with fertile soil.
interestingFact: Turmeric has been used in India for over 4,000 years.
research: Curcumin has been extensively studied for its antioxidant properties.
precautions: Large amounts may interact with blood-thinning medication.

name: Brahmi
description: Brahmi is a creeping medicinal herb traditionally used in Ayurveda to support memory, concentration and cognitive wellness.
habitat: Moist wetlands, ponds and tropical regions.
interestingFact: Brahmi has been used by Ayurvedic scholars for centuries to enhance learning.
research: Studies suggest Brahmi may support memory and cognitive performance.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before combining with sedative medications.

name: Giloy
description: Giloy, also known as Guduchi, is one of Ayurveda's most valued rejuvenating climbers and is traditionally used to support immunity and vitality.
habitat: Tropical forests where it climbs large trees.
interestingFact: Giloy is called 'Amrita', meaning the Root of Immortality.
research: Modern studies investigate its antioxidant and immune-supporting compounds.
precautions: Individuals with autoimmune disorders should seek professional advice before regular use.

name: Amla
description: Amla, or Indian Gooseberry, is one of Ayurveda's richest natural sources of Vitamin C and is highly regarded as a rejuvenating fruit.
habitat: Dry deciduous forests and tropical regions.
interestingFact: Amla is one of the three ingredients in the famous Ayurvedic formulation Triphala.
research: Research highlights its antioxidant and nutritional properties.
precautions: Individuals taking diabetes medication should consult a healthcare professional before regular consumption.

name: Moringa
description: Moringa is known as the Miracle Tree because almost every part of the plant is traditionally used for nutrition and wellness.
habitat: Warm tropical climates with well-drained soil.
interestingFact: Moringa leaves contain significant amounts of protein and essential nutrients.
research: Modern research investigates its nutritional and antioxidant potential.
precautions: Pregnant women should consult a healthcare professional before consuming certain parts of the plant.

name: Ginger
description: Ginger is a widely used medicinal rhizome in Ayurveda, valued for supporting digestion, circulation and overall wellness.
habitat: Warm humid tropical climates with fertile soil.
interestingFact: Ginger has been cultivated for over 5,000 years as both a spice and medicinal plant.
research: Extensively researched for digestive support and antioxidant properties.
precautions: People taking blood-thinning medication should consult a healthcare professional before regular use.

name: Garlic
description: Garlic has been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years and is valued for supporting cardiovascular and immune health.
benefits: May help maintain healthy blood pressure
habitat: Temperate climates with fertile, well-drained soil.
interestingFact: Ancient civilizations used garlic as both food and medicine.
research: Modern research focuses on allicin and cardiovascular health.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional if taking blood-thinning medication.

name: Licorice
description: Licorice root has long been used in Ayurveda to soothe the respiratory and digestive systems.
habitat: Warm temperate climates with fertile soil.
interestingFact: Licorice root is naturally much sweeter than sugar.
research: Studied for digestive and respiratory support.
precautions: Avoid excessive intake, especially with high blood pressure.

name: Cinnamon
description: Cinnamon is an aromatic spice traditionally valued for digestive support and warming properties.
interestingFact: True Cinnamon is harvested from the inner bark of young trees.
research: Studied for antioxidant and metabolic support.
precautions: Consume in moderation.

name: Clove
description: Clove flower buds are widely used in Ayurveda for oral health and digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Each clove is actually an unopened flower bud.
research: Widely researched for oral health applications.
precautions: Essential oil should be diluted before topical use.

name: Black Pepper
description: Known as the 'King of Spices', Black Pepper has been used in Ayurveda to enhance digestion and improve the absorption of nutrients.
interestingFact: Black pepper was once so valuable it was used as currency.
research: Piperine has been studied for improving nutrient absorption.
precautions: Consume in moderation if experiencing gastric irritation.

name: Cardamom
description: Cardamom is known as the 'Queen of Spices' and has been treasured in Ayurveda for its aromatic flavor and digestive benefits.
habitat: Humid tropical forests with rich fertile soil.
interestingFact: Cardamom is the world's third most expensive spice after saffron and vanilla.
research: Modern studies investigate its antioxidant and digestive-support properties.
precautions: Generally considered safe when consumed in moderate amounts.

name: Lemongrass
description: Lemongrass is an aromatic medicinal grass commonly used in Ayurveda for its refreshing properties and wellness benefits.
habitat: Warm tropical climates with abundant rainfall.
interestingFact: Lemongrass oil is widely used in aromatherapy.
research: Research focuses on antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds.
precautions: Essential oil should always be diluted before topical application.

name: Hibiscus
description: Hibiscus flowers have long been used in Ayurveda to support healthy hair, skin and cardiovascular wellness.
interestingFact: Hibiscus flowers are commonly used to prepare herbal teas around the world.
research: Modern studies investigate its antioxidant and cardiovascular-support properties.
precautions: Individuals taking blood pressure medication should seek medical advice before regular consumption.

name: Fenugreek
description: Fenugreek is widely used in Ayurvedic nutrition for supporting metabolism, digestion and overall vitality.
interestingFact: Fenugreek has been cultivated for over 6,000 years.
research: Current studies investigate its nutritional and metabolic benefits.
precautions: Pregnant women should consult a healthcare professional before medicinal use.

name: Mint
description: Mint is a refreshing medicinal herb traditionally used to support digestion, cooling and respiratory comfort.
habitat: Moist fertile soils in temperate climates.
interestingFact: There are more than 25 recognized species of mint worldwide.
research: Peppermint and spearmint continue to be studied for digestive and respiratory wellness.
precautions: Essential oil should be used only as directed.

name: Curry Leaves
description: Curry Leaves are an essential ingredient in Indian cuisine and have long been valued in Ayurveda for supporting digestion, metabolism and healthy hair.
habitat: Warm tropical climates with fertile soil.
interestingFact: Fresh curry leaves lose much of their aroma once dried.
research: Research investigates antioxidant and nutritional benefits.
precautions: Generally safe when consumed as food.

name: Bael
description: Bael is considered sacred in India and has traditionally been used to support digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Bael leaves are traditionally offered during worship of Lord Shiva.
research: Modern studies explore digestive and antioxidant properties.
precautions: Consume ripe fruit in moderation.

name: Arjuna
description: Arjuna bark has been valued for centuries in Ayurveda as a traditional herb supporting cardiovascular wellness.
interestingFact: The tree is named after the legendary warrior Arjuna from the Mahabharata.
research: Modern studies focus on cardiovascular support.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional if taking heart medication.

name: Shatavari
description: Shatavari is one of Ayurveda's most respected rejuvenating herbs, traditionally used to support vitality and women's wellness.
interestingFact: The name Shatavari means 'She who possesses a hundred husbands'.
research: Research explores adaptogenic and reproductive health properties.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before medicinal use during pregnancy.

name: Guduchi
description: Guduchi is a highly regarded rejuvenating vine in Ayurveda and is traditionally used to promote immunity and longevity.
habitat: Tropical forests where it grows as a climbing vine.
interestingFact: Guduchi and Giloy refer to the same medicinal plant in Ayurveda.
research: Widely studied for immune-supporting phytochemicals.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before prolonged medicinal use.

name: Saffron
description: Saffron is one of the world's most valuable medicinal spices, traditionally used in Ayurveda to promote vitality, mood, and overall wellness.
habitat: Cool temperate climates with well-drained soil.
interestingFact: Over 150,000 flowers are required to produce one kilogram of saffron.
research: Modern studies investigate mood-supporting and antioxidant properties.
precautions: Consume only in moderate quantities.

name: Indian Gooseberry
description: Indian Gooseberry, commonly known as Amla, is one of Ayurveda's most important rejuvenating fruits.
interestingFact: Amla is one of the three ingredients of Triphala.
research: Widely studied for antioxidant and nutritional benefits.
precautions: Consult a physician if managing blood sugar medication.

name: Sandalwood
description: Sandalwood is renowned for its cooling fragrance and has been used in Ayurveda, meditation and skincare for centuries.
interestingFact: Indian Sandalwood is among the world's most valuable woods.
research: Studied for aromatic and skin-supporting compounds.
precautions: Use essential oil only after proper dilution.

name: Drumstick
description: The Drumstick Tree is commonly known as the Miracle Tree because nearly every part is edible and nutritionally valuable.
interestingFact: Almost every part of the Moringa tree is usable.
research: Studied extensively for nutritional benefits.
precautions: Consume medicinal quantities only under professional guidance.

name: Holy Lotus
description: The Sacred Lotus is revered throughout Asia and has significant importance in Ayurveda, spirituality and traditional wellness.
interestingFact: Lotus seeds can remain viable for more than 1,000 years.
research: Modern research explores antioxidant and calming compounds.
precautions: Generally considered safe when consumed traditionally.

name: Bhringraj
description: Bhringraj is one of Ayurveda's most celebrated rejuvenating herbs, especially valued for supporting healthy hair, scalp, and liver function.
interestingFact: Bhringraj is commonly known as the 'King of Hair'.
research: Research focuses on hair follicle support and liver health.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before long-term medicinal use.

name: Kalmegh
description: Kalmegh is a bitter medicinal herb traditionally used in Ayurveda for supporting immunity and liver function.
habitat: Warm tropical regions with moderate rainfall.
interestingFact: Kalmegh is often called the 'King of Bitters'.
research: Widely studied for immune-supporting phytochemicals.
precautions: Avoid excessive use during pregnancy.

name: Manjistha
description: Manjistha is traditionally used in Ayurveda to support healthy skin and natural detoxification.
interestingFact: Its roots have been used for centuries as a natural red dye.
research: Current research explores antioxidant and skin-supporting compounds.
precautions: Seek medical advice before prolonged medicinal use.

name: Haritaki
description: Haritaki is one of Ayurveda's most revered rejuvenating fruits and forms an important ingredient of Triphala.
interestingFact: Haritaki is often called the 'King of Medicines' in Ayurveda.
research: Modern studies investigate antioxidant and digestive properties.
precautions: Avoid excessive medicinal use during pregnancy.

name: Bibhitaki
description: Bibhitaki is another important fruit in Triphala and has traditionally been used to support respiratory and digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Bibhitaki is one of the three fruits that make up Triphala.
research: Current studies explore antioxidant and digestive benefits.
precautions: Use medicinally only under professional guidance.

name: Triphala
description: Triphala is a classical Ayurvedic formulation consisting of Haritaki, Bibhitaki and Amla. It is traditionally used to promote digestion, detoxification and overall vitality.
interestingFact: Triphala has been used in Ayurveda for more than 2,000 years.
research: Modern studies investigate its antioxidant and digestive-support properties.
precautions: Consume under professional guidance for long-term medicinal use.

name: Yashtimadhu
description: Yashtimadhu, commonly known as Licorice, is traditionally valued for respiratory and digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Yashtimadhu is naturally sweeter than sugar.
research: Widely researched for digestive and respiratory support.
precautions: Avoid excessive intake if managing hypertension.

name: Shankhpushpi
description: Shankhpushpi is traditionally used to support memory, concentration and mental wellbeing.
interestingFact: Its flowers resemble a conch shell, giving the herb its name.
research: Current studies investigate cognitive-support properties.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before prolonged medicinal use.

name: Punarnava
description: Punarnava is a rejuvenating herb traditionally used in Ayurveda to support kidney and liver wellness.
interestingFact: Punarnava means 'renewed again' in Sanskrit.
research: Research investigates renal-support compounds.
precautions: Use medicinally under professional guidance.

name: Vacha
description: Vacha is an aromatic marsh plant traditionally used to support speech, memory and cognitive function.
habitat: Wetlands, marshes and shallow freshwater bodies.
interestingFact: Vacha has been mentioned in Ayurvedic texts for thousands of years.
research: Modern studies investigate its neurological properties.
precautions: Should only be used under qualified professional supervision.

name: Guggul
description: Guggul is a resin-producing shrub traditionally used in Ayurveda to support joints, metabolism and cardiovascular wellness.
interestingFact: Guggul resin has been used in Ayurveda for over 2,500 years.
research: Modern studies investigate metabolic and cardiovascular support.
precautions: Consult a physician before medicinal use.

name: Nirgundi
description: Nirgundi is traditionally valued for supporting muscles, joints and respiratory wellness.
interestingFact: Its leaves are traditionally warmed and applied externally.
research: Research explores anti-inflammatory phytochemicals.
precautions: External medicinal use should follow professional guidance.

name: Jatamansi
description: Jatamansi is a Himalayan herb traditionally used for relaxation and mental wellness.
interestingFact: Jatamansi grows above 3,000 metres.
research: Research focuses on calming and neurological properties.
precautions: Use only under professional supervision.

name: Patha
description: Patha is a medicinal climbing plant traditionally used to support digestion and urinary wellness.
interestingFact: Patha has been described in ancient Ayurvedic texts for centuries.
research: Modern studies investigate its phytochemical composition.
precautions: Use medicinally under guidance.

name: Kachnar
description: Kachnar is a flowering tree valued in Ayurveda for supporting glandular and digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Its flowers resemble orchids.
research: Research explores antioxidant properties.
precautions: Consult a healthcare professional before medicinal use.

name: Anantamul
description: Anantamul is traditionally used as a cooling herb that supports healthy skin and natural detoxification.
interestingFact: Its roots possess a pleasant vanilla-like fragrance.
research: Current studies investigate antioxidant activity.
precautions: Consume medicinally under professional supervision.

name: Chirata
description: Chirata is one of Ayurveda's classic bitter herbs traditionally used to support digestion and immunity.
interestingFact: Chirata is among Ayurveda's most bitter herbs.
research: Modern studies explore antioxidant phytochemicals.
precautions: Consume under qualified guidance.

name: Vidanga
description: Vidanga is a traditional Ayurvedic climbing shrub known for digestive and cleansing applications.
interestingFact: Vidanga berries have been used in Ayurveda for centuries.
research: Current research investigates bioactive compounds.
precautions: Use only as advised by professionals.

name: Kokum
description: Kokum is a tropical fruit traditionally used as a cooling food and digestive support.
interestingFact: Kokum butter is also used in skincare.
research: Research explores antioxidant compounds such as garcinol.
precautions: Generally safe as food.

name: Karela
description: Karela, or Bitter Gourd, is widely used in Ayurveda and traditional nutrition for supporting metabolism and digestive wellness.
interestingFact: Despite its bitterness, Karela is cultivated throughout Asia for both food and traditional wellness.
research: Modern research investigates its nutritional and metabolic properties.
precautions: Individuals managing blood sugar should consult a healthcare professional before medicinal use.
